title: Trading Information | Trading at NYSE
summary: This is a static reference page providing operational trading information for NYSE venues, including trading hours, auction procedures, and market integrity mechanisms such as Limit Up/Limit Down (LULD) and Market-Wide Circuit Breakers (MWCB). The page covers NYSE, NYSE American, NYSE Arca, NYSE National, and NYSE Texas trading schedules.
key_points: NYSE Tape A core trading session runs 9:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. ET with Opening Auction at 9:30 a.m. and Closing Auction at 4:00 p.m.
NYSE Arca Equities has the earliest pre-opening session at 2:30 a.m. ET with Early Open Auction at 4:00 a.m.
NYSE American and NYSE Texas offer Late Trading Sessions from 4:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m. ET.
Limit Up/Limit Down (LULD) Plan was approved permanently on April 11, 2019 to prevent trades outside specified price bands.
Market-Wide Circuit Breakers (MWCB) have three thresholds: 7% (Level 1), 13% (Level 2), and 20% (Level 3) based on S&P 500 single-day decline.
Clearly Erroneous Execution review requests must be submitted within 30 minutes of execution time.
why_it_matters: This page documents the operational framework of NYSE trading venues, including auction mechanisms, trading hours across multiple platforms, and volatility control systems. It provides the baseline rules and timing structure for equity and options trading on NYSE markets.
market_structure_context: The page details NYSE market structure elements including: pre-opening order queuing, opening and closing auction procedures, imbalance freeze periods, MOO/LOO cancellation cutoffs, circuit breaker mechanisms (LULD and MWCB), and clearly erroneous execution review procedures. NYSE Bonds conducts two daily auctions at 4:00 a.m. and 8:00 a.m. ET.
important_numbers: 15 minutes CEE review window for non-customer American/Arca Options orders
